findings.title: ToastContainer uses inline SVGs instead of LucideIcon
findings.description: The ToastContainer script block still uses hardcoded inline SVG strings for toast icons (CheckCircle, XCircle, Info, AlertTriangle) instead of the LucideIcon component. This violates AC-US1-03.
findings.evidence: Lines 34-38 contain SVG strings: `success: '<svg class="w-5 h-5" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24">...'`. There are also duplicate typeStyles/typeIcons definitions - frontmatter has one set (lines 5-17) that's completely unused.
findings.impact: Users see inconsistent icon rendering. The unified LucideIcon component is not being used as specified.
findings.recommendation: 1. Remove duplicate frontmatter definitions (lines 5-17). 2. Replace inline SVG strings with LucideIcon calls. Since toasts are rendered dynamically in JavaScript, consider either: (a) Create a helper function that returns SVG strings for server-side rendering, or (b) Use data attributes to trigger client-side icon replacement after render.

findings.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion media query support
findings.description: The spec (FR-003) explicitly requires respecting the prefers-reduced-motion media query, but the MotionAnimations.astro auto-initialization does not check for this preference. All animations run unconditionally.
findings.evidence: The DOMContentLoaded handler at line 518 runs all animations without checking `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')`.
findings.impact: Users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity may experience discomfort. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level AA requirement.
findings.recommendation: Add a reduced motion check at the start of DOMContentLoaded: `const prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;` Then conditionally skip animations or use instant transitions when true. Also wrap any CSS animations with `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)`.

findings.title: Missing ARIA attributes for toggle state
findings.description: The theme toggle button lacks ARIA attributes to communicate its state to screen readers. It should indicate whether dark mode is currently active.
findings.evidence: Button has only `aria-label="Toggle theme"` but no `aria-pressed` or `aria-checked` attribute to indicate current theme state.
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot determine the current theme state from the toggle.
findings.recommendation: Add `aria-pressed={isDark}` dynamically, or use `role="switch"` with `aria-checked={isDark}`. Update the JS to toggle this attribute along with the visual state.

findings.title: Dropdown buttons missing ARIA attributes
findings.description: The dropdown trigger buttons ('Business' and 'Govs & NGOs') lack ARIA attributes to indicate they control expandable content.
findings.evidence: Lines 29-32: `<button>Business <ChevronDown/></button>` - no aria-expanded, aria-haspopup, or aria-controls.
findings.impact: Screen readers don't know these buttons control dropdown menus.
findings.recommendation: Add: `aria-expanded="false"` (toggle via JS), `aria-haspopup="menu"`, and `aria-controls="dropdown-business"` (with matching IDs on dropdown divs). Update aria-expanded on hover.

findings.title: Silent fallback to HelpCircle may hide errors
findings.description: The component falls back to HelpCircle for invalid icon names. While this prevents crashes, it may silently mask typos during development.
findings.evidence: Line 18: `LucideIcons[name as keyof typeof LucideIcons] || LucideIcons['HelpCircle']`
findings.impact: A typo like 'Buildng2' instead of 'Building2' would render HelpCircle with no visible error in development.
findings.recommendation: Consider: (1) Add console.warn for invalid names in development mode, (2) Use TypeScript to validate props at compile time if @lucide/astro supports it, or (3) Document this behavior in a comment.

findings.title: Correct XSS protection but consider safer pattern
findings.description: The escapeHtml function correctly prevents XSS, but the innerHTML pattern could be simplified by using textContent for the message.
findings.evidence: Lines 41-43 define escapeHtml, line 53 uses it: `${escapeHtml(t.message)}`.
findings.impact: Current implementation is safe but slightly harder to audit. Using textContent would be simpler.
findings.recommendation: Consider using `span.textContent = t.message` instead of innerHTML interpolation, which removes the need for manual escaping entirely.

findings.title: Duplicate escapeHtml function definition
findings.description: Both Header.astro and ToastContainer.astro define the same escapeHtml function. This is duplicated code that should be extracted.
findings.impact: Maintenance overhead if the function needs to change. Minor bundle size impact.
findings.recommendation: Extract escapeHtml to a shared utility file like `src/lib/sanitize.ts` or `src/lib/dom-utils.ts` and import it in both components.

findings.title: CSS keyframes not wrapped in prefers-reduced-motion
findings.description: The MotionAnimations CSS includes keyframes (shimmer, ripple, float, pageIn) that will still animate when prefers-reduced-motion is set.
findings.evidence: Lines 646-711 define CSS animations that run regardless of user preferences.
findings.impact: CSS-based animations don't respect accessibility preferences.
findings.recommendation: Wrap animation definitions in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) { /* animations */ }` to disable them when users prefer reduced motion.

findings.title: Server-side check for auth state before client hydration
findings.description: Line 11 checks sessionStorage/localStorage on the server, which will always be false during SSR since these APIs don't exist server-side.
findings.impact: Minor - the check works but is redundant. The `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard is always false during SSR, so isLoggedIn will always be false on the server. The client-side updateAuthState() handles the real check.
findings.recommendation: Simplify to `const isLoggedIn = false;` for SSR, or remove the variable entirely since updateAuthState() re-renders auth UI client-side anyway.

findings.title: Animation initialization duplicated across files
findings.description: The index.astro script has its own hero animation setup (lines 129-133) that duplicates functionality already in MotionAnimations.astro auto-init (lines 519-522).
findings.evidence: index.astro calls `textReveal(heroTitle, 0.5)` in its own DOMContentLoaded, while MotionAnimations.astro auto-init calls `textReveal(heroTitle, 0.3)` on all h1 elements.
findings.impact: Text will be processed twice (double word splitting) on the homepage h1. This could cause visual glitches.
findings.recommendation: Remove the duplicate textReveal call from index.astro (lines 130-133) and let MotionAnimations.astro handle it, adjusting the delay in the auto-init if needed.
